summary:
The agent concludes that neither depth cuts, visual inspection, wax chew-in nor articulating paper has direct supporting evidence in the retrieved documentation regarding occlusal clearance detection in onlays. Despite lacking specific validation within the available resources, the agent ultimately defaults to recommending articulating paper due to its established role in assessing dynamic occlusion in dental practice.  

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{D}}
